reviews.review_text: this game makes me feel like a complete and utter genius, fr makes you feel like napoleon. massive ego boost
its good i got 80 hours of gameplay split across multiple campains and i really did have lots of fun there are some issues with the ai its not very smart but for a somewhat old game its not that bad
Great game for fans of the Period, Mods are suggested although, cause the game is very old and outdated. But lots of love were put into the game, definitely!
As a total war fan this game even after all the years still holds up in the multiplayer. If you like the time period and the mechanic of line battles this is a game for you!
Napoleon total war is an overall big improvement to Empire total war. If you liked Empire you'll surely like  Napoleon definitive edition.
Shockingly good in spite of it's age find a couple of friends willing to suffer through the modding process and there's a ton of content some of which is still being updated (FCN!)
It's old (I am writing this in 2026), but it is one of my favorite games. The battles with gunpowder are way more fun that anything else in the TW franchise.
This game is best with mods like NTW3 but the Vanilla version is good too

Great atmosphere and dynamic long-paced battles heavily focused on proper maneuvering. Would recommend playing with Darth Mod for much larger scale battles, and for unlocking the minor factions as playable nations.

Only real negative is that they disabled in-game chat for multiplayer, which pretty much killed the online scene. They did that for every TW game though, so it is what it is...
good game, graphics old enough for my potato laptop to run it

As an overall fan of the Total War series, this game captivates my love for it perfectly. From updated combat systems, more inclusion for naval warfare, updated replenishment system, and much more. Overall; compared to the original Total War: Empire, this one is much better.

So I give this game a 7 out of 10. I like the mechanics and think it's better gameplay wise than empire total war. However I remember an issue back in the day where the game would become more laggy and choppy as you progressed through your campaign. I wonder if that was ever fixed? Either way I know Total War is putting out the new 40k game, but a musket era total war is needed big time. Let's do the American Civil War.
yes but i wish they added a bit to install mods from steam cant install mods but peak game
ok but empire total war has more replayability because napolen total war is only europe, but it fixes all the bad sides of empire total war better ai and its better in everything
Fun game mods are a bit dodgy to get given you have to visit old websites to download files.
NTW3 is fun as cav corps but as cav corps alone.

Even though this game is already pretty old I still love it and it's my favourite Total War. I definitely recommend buying this game especially if you like the napoleonic era and you are looking for an RTS game.

The game have all the pros of Empire TW but never perfect them, and the cons of ETW and RTW, but never resolve them while laying the foundations for another bad successor, R2TW.
Oddly, the finished version of Napoleon Total War is Shogun 2 Fall of the Samurai.
If this game was polished a bit more in Medieval Total War 2's direction, it would have been a great game.

I was wishing for this game when it's first came out(yes, a loooooooooong time ago), but never got a chance to play it until I have the Total War bundles. Even then, I keep forgetting about Napoleon while playing the other TW.
Now, when I have time to experience my teen-hood game-of-wish, it's quite disappointing.

Honestly, I like the game, but can not tolerate its flaws, as a TW fan.

The musket line-fire mechanic is very bad, worse than Shogun 2 and Empire TW; which is unacceptable for a pre-modern strategy game.
American Conquest did it flawlessly as a RTS game for Windows Xp a decade before while NTW AI keeps surrendering or melee-ing after one or 2 rounds of volleys.

With mods, I still avoid Battle Mode and use Auto Resolve to avoid the game's combat.
I enjoy this game economy system, somehow.
Please bring back this era of TW. Napoleon is great, its smaller scale than Empire, but so smooth!
A true classic that still holds up in 2026. An absolute must for any fan of Napoleonic era warfare.

Empire benim için o kadarda iyi bir oyun olmadı ama Napoleon genişleme paketi gibi çok kapsamlı bir oyun olmasada daha oturaklı daha oynaması eğlenceli bir oyun. En iyi 2. veya 3. total war oyunu Rome 2 ve bunun arasında bir seçim yapmak zor ama birinci kesinlikle Medival 2. (Bu arada the great war modunu internetten indirip kurmanızı öneririm oyunu tamamen 1. dünya savaşına çeviriyor gerçekten inanılmaz bir mod)

Its mediocre at most, needs mods to fix it:
(top is most relevant and bottom is least)

1. The game suffers from horrible graphics, weird buggy sounds that break your ears that happen at any given time.

2. Units are trash because they have no energy and in no time they are exhausted (COMPLETELY INACCURATE) maneuverability is trash (again).

3. It does not make an extra effort to be accurate, unit sizes are tiny and pathetic, the biggest ones are 120 men which is inaccurate and in empire total war its 200 and the graphics arent such garbage.

4. The devs clearly were just lazy and looking for easy money, either that or completely incompetent.

Some minor positives: 

Its decent when it comes to general information and vibes, but even that is not good because the whole point is either accuracy with specified units and uniforms, or the scale of battles, neither of which they do a good job at.

I give it a 4/10 at most, after having played the Italian campaign, and part of the egypt campaign, im already fed up with it, and its such a shame honestly. I love the Napoleonic era and this is such a fall from grace of the actual scale and grandeur that could be implemented, that the modders have had to do for the lazy corporate devs that put such little effort in comparison, its just a big trashy copy paste from empire total war or viseversa (I dont care) and its worse somehow??? Im baffled, I dont care how old it is, if empire is enjoyable and has decent scale then how can this be such trash???

Overall I cannot recommend it for people who like the history or want to enjoy, its not enjoyable, and its not worth the price unless huge discount and if you add mods, (which by the way is super hard).
Fighting in Spain is complicated and fun
"Between a battle won, and a battle lost, the distance is immense; and there stand empires."

Ive played exactly one playthrough as Russia, but Ive also logged 850 hours on Total War Empire so here's what you need to know

#1 = European Focus... In TW:Empire if you considered the Atlantic colonies a pain in the *** and you always got annoyed at the Martha Confederacy coming in and screwing with your late game, that is not an issue anymore. Map spans from Portugal to Moscow, Sicily to Sweden. You dont even have to deal with North Africa anymore, all the regions that were in TW:Empire outside of Europe are now just trade routes you can choose to invest in or ignore. There's also a few more regions that have been added to the European map in France, Italy, England, Spain, etc, which makes completing a grand campaign still have weight to it like TW:Empire

#2 = Pacing Changes... TW:Empire I believe had turns last about once every 4 months to follow seasonal changes (Fall, Winter, Spring, Summer). TW:Napoleon on the other hand has a shorter time frame where turns last about 2-3 months... Because of that, and this is where the biggest downside of the game kicks in, IT TAKES MUCH LONGER BUILDING SHIPS AND UPGRADING SETTLEMENTS IN THIS GAME THAN TW:EMPIRE. High end ships and important buildings can easily take 8-10 turns to complete, whereas TW:Empire's was closer to 6. This can GREATLY impact navy lovers since it takes much longer to put together fleets and also unlock end-game units that require upgraded buildings to research. 

#3 = Streamlined... You don't have to deal with religion in TW:Napoleon the way you had to with TW:Empire. No missionaries running around anymore, the AI does not become obsessed with building churches at every spot of land, Im not sure if there is a happiness penalty with conquering cities if you are a different religion anymore, but if there is I failed to notice it in my playthrough, so if you didnt like the religion stuff in TW:Empire and thought it was a hinderance, good news, you dont have to deal with that anymore. Same with pirate fleets spawning and wrecking trade routes in TW:Empire, they are not present or anything you have to deal with in TW:Napoleon. 

#4 = Building Swapping... A very pleasant surprise I had in my playthrough is the ability of switch buildings from one type to another. In TW:Empire if you build a college in a land spot and want to replace it with a workshop, you would have to tear down the whole building and build the new building from scratch. In TW:Napoleon you can now convert a building from one type to another (5-8 turns) rather than tear down and build up from scratch, which is a HUGE deal when it comes to ports. Trade ports or military ports was constantly a decision I struggled with when playing TW:Empire, but TW:Napoleon makes me have to sweat about it far less than in the past. This applied to almost all buildings in the game in both towns and cities too which is just (chef's kiss) 

#5 = Late Game Performance... The main thing that finally killed TW:Empire for me was how in the late game you could run into some game-crashing bugs that kicks you off of the game, and no number of reloading autosaves or playing things differently can get around the crashing. (I have changed my computer in between TW:Empire and TW:Napoleon which may also have something to do with that in fairness). That being said in TW:Napoleon my first campaign plowed through 300 turns and I didn't have any slowdown or computer overheating or any technical issue start rearing its head. 

Overall, if TW:Empire has been giving you headaches or throwing problems at you that turns you off from playing it, but still really love the time period in history within the game, TW:Napoleon can be the perfect remedy to help you scratch that itch the way TW:Empire almost does.
